seo_title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
meta_description: Researchers develop a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation, improving trust and safety in AI models.
body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers have developed a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

Predictions that return to the same solution are deemed confident, while those that change substantially are flagged as uncertain. The approach improves trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than baselines. Resilience leverages the intrinsic iterative structure of NCAs by probing the stability of the final prediction under small perturbations of the automaton state.

riangleDice@90 and AURC Selective prediction metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

AUROC and AUPRC Ranking metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

Advantages over Baselines Resilience identifies failure cases more reliably than baselines, improving trust and safety in NCA-based models.

Across multiple medical segmentation benchmarks, resilience has shown promising results, outperforming baseline approaches.

Read the full paper to learn more about this innovative approach to uncertainty estimation in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

source.title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method to measure the uncertainty of predictions made by neural cellular automata (NCA), a lightweight alternative for medical image segmentation. The approach, called "resilience," assesses the stability of final predictions under small perturbations of the automaton state, flagging uncertain predictions that change substantially. This method has been shown to improve trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than existing baselines.
